novel_features.name: Dead-link detection
novel_features.description: Find archived, expired, or zero-traffic links across the workspace before they pile up.
novel_features.rationale: Joins local link metadata with analytics aggregates that the API doesn't expose together.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to clean up dormant short links before a portfolio review or before bulk-archiving. The /analytics endpoint can't filter links by 'no clicks in N days' in a single call.

novel_features.name: Performance drift detection
novel_features.description: Detect links whose click rate dropped more than threshold percent week-over-week.
novel_features.rationale: Requires sequential analytics snapshots stored locally. The /analytics endpoint returns point-in-time data, not deltas.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Catches dying campaigns before reporting deadlines. Use this in a weekly automation to surface attribution links that quietly stopped converting.

novel_features.name: Duplicate destination finder
novel_features.description: Find every link in the workspace pointing to the same destination URL.
novel_features.rationale: Pure SQL self-join over the local link store. The API has no aggregation endpoint for grouping by destination.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Surfaces accidental duplicates from bulk-create overruns and consolidation candidates after a migration.

novel_features.name: Slug-collision lint
novel_features.description: Audit short-key slugs for lookalike collisions, reserved-word violations, and brand-conflict hazards.
novel_features.rationale: Pure local-data audit — no API endpoint to ask whether '/launch' and '/launches' are confusing.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before a brand campaign launch to catch lookalike slugs that confuse partners or get reserved-word treatment.

novel_features.name: Bulk URL/UTM rewrite with diff
novel_features.description: Show every link that would change and the exact patch BEFORE sending.
novel_features.rationale: Mass UTM or domain migrations need dry-run safety. The bulk-update endpoint accepts the patch but doesn't preview.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before any campaign-wide rewrite. Diff preview prevents the worst class of bulk-mutation mistakes.

novel_features.name: Tag/folder-grouped rollup
novel_features.description: Performance dashboard aggregated by tag or folder — clicks, leads, sales rolled up across every link wearing each label.
novel_features.rationale: Joins analytics_buckets × links × tags × folders locally. The /analytics endpoint groups by top_links but cannot join with local taxonomy in one call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to compare campaign performance across tag dimensions without reconciling 5 separate API calls.

novel_features.name: Conversion funnel
novel_features.description: Click-to-lead-to-sale conversion rates per link or campaign.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining /events with /track/lead and /track/sale state stored locally over time. The API surfaces individual events but not ratios across windows.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Surfaces where prospects drop off in your attribution funnel. Use before quarterly reporting to spot links with high clicks and low conversion.

novel_features.name: Customer journey timeline
novel_features.description: See every link a customer clicked, when they became a lead, and when they purchased — in one timeline.
novel_features.rationale: Joins customer record × events × track/lead × track/sale across local store. The API offers no per-customer timeline endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for QBR-style account reviews or to debug attribution issues for a specific customer.

novel_features.name: Partner leaderboard
novel_features.description: Rank partners by commission earned, conversion rate, and clicks generated.
novel_features.rationale: Joins /partners/analytics with local commissions × payouts for per-partner ROI accurate to the latest payout cycle.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to identify top performers before a partner-tier review, or dormant partners worth deactivating.

novel_features.name: Commission audit reconciliation
novel_features.description: Reconcile partners, commissions, bounties, and payouts to flag stale rates, missing payouts, and expired bounties still earning.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-resource join only possible with all data side-by-side in the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run this before a payout cycle to catch billing surprises. Use in CI before any commission-rate migration.

novel_features.name: Workspace health doctor
novel_features.description: Cross-resource Monday-morning report: rate-limit headroom, expired-but-active links, dead destination URLs, unverified domains, dormant tags, bounty submissions awaiting review.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates signals across 6 resource families that no single API endpoint surfaces together.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this as the first thing every morning, or as a CI canary. Surfaces what needs attention without dashboard hopping.

novel_features.name: Time-windowed change feed
novel_features.description: What happened in the last N hours? Created, updated, deleted links plus partner approvals, new bounty submissions, and top-clicked entities.
novel_features.rationale: Requires local timestamps. Powers agent 'what's new' flows that the API can't answer in a single call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this in agent loops to summarize workspace activity since the last check-in. Cheap and idempotent.

novel_features.name: Bounty submission triage queue
novel_features.description: Group partner-submitted bounty proof by status, age, and bounty type. Surfaces backlog awaiting review.
novel_features.rationale: Joins /bounties/{id}/submissions across all bounties locally. The API requires per-bounty pagination — the triage view requires aggregation across them.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run weekly to keep bounty submissions from rotting. Bounty programs lose partner trust when submissions sit unreviewed.

novel_features.name: Bounty payout projection
novel_features.description: Project upcoming payouts from approved-but-unpaid submissions multiplied by current commission rates.
novel_features.rationale: Joins bounties × commissions × payouts locally to produce a forecast that no single API endpoint exposes.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for finance/marketing planning. Surfaces upcoming payout liability before the next payout cycle.

narrative.headline: Every Dub feature, plus offline search, agent-native output, and a local SQLite store no other Dub tool has.
narrative.value_prop: dub-pp-cli covers all 53 Dub API operations as typed commands, then transcends with cross-resource joins the API cannot answer alone — dead-link detection, drift detection, partner leaderboards from /partners/analytics joined with local commissions, bounty submission triage, and a Monday-morning workspace health report. The official dub-cli covers 6 commands; we cover 67.
narrative.auth_narrative: dub-pp-cli reads DUB_API_KEY from the environment (Speakeasy convention; DUB_TOKEN also accepted for compatibility with prior community CLIs). The key is workspace-scoped — the workspace is implicit in the key. Get one from dub.co/settings/tokens and run `dub-pp-cli doctor` to verify connectivity.

quickstart.comment: Verify API key, base URL, and rate-limit headroom.
Populate the local SQLite store so search and transcendence commands work offline.
List the workspace's links with the fields agents care about.
Find dormant links — the headline transcendence feature.
Cross-resource Monday-morning report: rate-limit headroom, expiring links, bounty triage backlog.

troubleshoots.fix: Confirm DUB_API_KEY is set and starts with `dub_`. Tokens from dub.co/settings/tokens are workspace-scoped.
Analytics has tighter per-second caps (Pro 2/s, Advanced 8/s). The client retries with backoff; pass `--page-size 50` to reduce throughput.

troubleshoots.symptom: search returns nothing after fresh install
troubleshoots.fix: Run `dub-pp-cli sync` once to populate the local store. Search queries the local FTS index, not the live API.

troubleshoots.symptom: links rewrite blasted too many links
troubleshoots.fix: Always use `--dry-run` first. The diff preview shows every link that would change before any mutation.

narrative.when_to_use: Use dub-pp-cli when an agent or operator needs to manage Dub at workspace scale — bulk link operations, campaign-wide UTM rewrites, partner program audits, bounty triage, or local cross-resource analysis. The CLI's local SQLite store makes joins across links, analytics, partners, commissions, and bounties cheap and offline. Reach for dub-pp-cli over the official `dub-cli` when you need anything beyond link shortening or workspace config.

recipes.title: Find dormant links worth archiving
recipes.command: dub-pp-cli sync && dub-pp-cli links stale --days 60 --json --select key,url,clicks,createdAt
recipes.explanation: Sync first, then ask for links with zero clicks in the last 60 days. Pipe through `jq` to filter further or build an archive batch.

recipes.title: Preview a campaign-wide UTM swap before sending
recipes.explanation: The diff preview shows every link that would change. Drop `--dry-run` only after reading the patch.

recipes.title: Triage bounty submissions waiting more than 7 days
recipes.explanation: Surfaces the partner submissions rotting in the queue. Pipe through `jq` to group by `partnerId` for outreach lists.

recipes.title: Per-partner ROI for the top 10 partners
recipes.explanation: Combines /partners/analytics with local commissions and payouts. Use `--select` with dotted paths to keep the response small for agents — leaderboards return wide rows by default.

recipes.title: Sync, then run the full Monday-morning health check
recipes.explanation: Combined report across rate-limit headroom, expired-but-active links, dead destination URLs, unverified domains, and bounty submissions awaiting review.
